what_it_does: Manages loading, unloading, status polling, and persona switching for loadable voice services by reading ops/services.json, driving systemd user units, probing health endpoints, and delegating VRAM budgeting and teardown to a shared ops/cli resource-manager core.
observations: Reads the canonical service registry (ops/services.json) and treats any group:'voice' entry with a 'load' block as loadable.
Imports ops/cli modules (gpu, systemd, registry) by inserting ops/cli into sys.path, then delegates all budget/teardown/unit-control calls to them.
Uses systemd unit state as the authoritative 'has it started' signal and the health endpoint as the 'is it warm' discriminator.
Writes a /tmp marker file per service on load start; poll_wake() consumes the marker when the service first answers health to compute measured wake time in ms.
unload() uses the shared gpu.teardown path, sleeps 1.5s, then verifies the port is closed and warns if the service still appears resident.
switch_to() evicts all other resident tts-* engines before loading the target persona voice.
Idempotent where possible: already-up loads and switches return immediately without relaunching.
standouts: Explicit convergence narrative: replaces a private Popen/pgrep lifecycle with the same systemd units and shared VRAM authority the company console uses.
Orphan-safe teardown reasoning: systemd cgroup stop is chosen specifically to reap vLLM EngineCore processes that survive launcher SIGTERM.
G7-loadcost telemetry via cheap /tmp wall-clock markers, kept outside any SUITE dependency so the module stays stdlib-only.
Fail-loud budget gate that names free-vs-need, current card holders, and candidate evictions before starting a unit.
conventions: Heavy docstrings that narrate design history and rationale, not just API contracts.
Single-source-of-truth: no hardcoded port/venv/vram map; everything comes from services.json.
Stdlib-only in the bridge interpreter; service code runs in isolated per-service venvs managed by systemd.
Fail-loud for load/unload decisions that risk OOM or orphan processes; fail-soft for status rendering when VRAM read hiccups.
Idempotent operations with explicit 'already resident' short-circuits.
concerns: sys.path mutation at module import time could shadow other modules named gpu/systemd/registry in the bridge interpreter.
/tmp load-start markers are not user-namespaced and could collide across users or leak if poll_wake never sees the service come up.
unload() sleeps a fixed 1.5s before verifying VRAM, which is a coarse heuristic and may race with slow/failed teardown.
No concurrency guard: concurrent load()/unload()/switch_to() calls could interleave unit control and budget checks.
The marker write is wrapped in a bare except pass, so a failed telemetry stamp is silently lost.
notes: A bridge-level voice lifecycle coordinator whose main job is to stay out of the way: it asks the shared resource manager and systemd for truth instead of keeping its own pid tables or budgets.
questions: What exact return values can _sd.is_active() produce, and how does it distinguish 'activating' from 'active'?
What fields are required inside the services.json 'load' block (port, venv, vram_mb, kind)?
How are systemd user-unit files generated and kept in sync with services.json entries?
What is the G7-loadcost spec, and where does the emitted run-record go after poll_wake() returns data?
How does _gpu.check_fit() measure or estimate per-service VRAM, and what does the third tuple element represent?
What happens if a service's health endpoint returns a non-2xx HTTP status below 500 — is that truly 'up'?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a voice-scoped slice of the shared VRAM/resource manager that loads/unloads voice services via systemd units and reports status.
apparent_themes: convergence of UI and console control planes
intention_signals: The file is trying to eliminate a previously real dual-authority problem where UI-launched voice services were invisible to the company console's VRAM budget, by routing every launch and teardown through the same systemd units and shared gpu core.
novelty: The unusually explicit 'convergence' narrative embedded in comments and docstrings, plus the specific reasoning that systemd cgroup teardown is the orphan-safe path for vLLM EngineCore.
contribution: A voice-specific lifecycle facade that the fused system would keep because it translates persona/engine names into service ids, swaps resident TTS voices safely, measures actual wake times, and channels all budget and teardown decisions through the shared ops/cli core rather than inventing a private one.
summary_for_embedding: A stdlib-only Python module that loads, unloads, and reports status for loadable voice services by reading services.json, driving systemd user units, probing health endpoints, and gating VRAM through a shared resource manager.
intention_for_embedding: To converge UI-driven voice service lifecycle management onto the same systemd units and shared VRAM authority used by the company console, preventing dual-authority OOMs and orphan processes while measuring actual service wake times.

symbols.does: Returns a dict of all loadable voice services from services.json, keyed by id, including their load spec, health path, title, and full service record.
symbols.description: This function opens ops/services.json and filters for entries whose group is 'voice' and whose 'load' key is a dict. It returns enough context (including the whole svc record) for the shared core to budget, control, and teardown each service. It enforces the 'registry is truth' design by refusing to hardcode a service list.

symbols.does: Builds the base local HTTP URL for a loadable service from its port.
symbols.description: Given a load spec containing a 'port', returns 'http://127.0.0.1:{port}'. This is the local root used by is_up() when appending the service's health path.

symbols.does: Reads current GPU memory state via the shared gpu core and returns it in the shape callers expect.
symbols.description: Calls _gpu.read_gpu() and, if successful, returns used_mb, free_mb, total_mb, and util_pct. If the read fails it raises RuntimeError with a loud message rather than assuming the card has room.

symbols.does: Probes a service's health endpoint and returns True if it answers as live.
symbols.description: Performs a GET to _url(load) + the service's health path with a 3-second timeout. HTTP errors with code below 500 and 2xx/3xx/4xx responses are treated as up; any network or 5xx error is treated as down. This separates a merely 'active' systemd unit from a fully warmed model.

symbols.does: Returns the state (up/warming/down) and VRAM snapshot for every loadable voice service.
symbols.description: For each loadable voice service it checks is_up() first, then falls back to _sd.is_active() to decide between 'up', 'warming', and 'down'. VRAM is read via vram() but failures are captured as an error string so the picker can still render.

symbols.does: Starts a voice service's systemd unit after passing a shared VRAM budget gate.
symbols.description: Validates the service id, checks the venv exists, confirms the service has a systemd unit, runs _gpu.check_fit() through the shared core, and then calls _sd.control(..., 'start'). On success it writes a /tmp load-start marker for wake-time measurement and returns state 'warming'. It is idempotent for already-up services.

symbols.does: Returns the /tmp marker file path used to record a service's load start time.
symbols.description: Maps a service id to '/tmp/company-voice-{service_id}.loadstart'. This file stores a wall-clock timestamp so poll_wake() can compute measured wake time.

symbols.does: Detects services that have just become healthy and reports their measured wake time.
symbols.description: Iterates loadable services, looks for an existing load-start marker, checks is_up(), and if warm computes wake_ms as the elapsed time since the marker. It appends the current card used_mb and deletes the marker so each wake is recorded exactly once. It returns data only; SUITE emission is left to the bridge caller.

symbols.does: Stops a voice service via the shared teardown path and verifies it actually went down.
symbols.description: Calls _gpu.teardown(svc) to perform a cgroup-safe systemd stop, waits 1.5s, then checks whether the port is still open. If the port still answers it returns a loud warning about a possible orphan squatting VRAM instead of blindly killing GPU pids. It is idempotent and fail-loud for unknown ids.

symbols.does: Maps a TTS engine name to its services.json id if it is loadable.
symbols.description: Returns 'tts-{engine}' when that id exists in _loadable(), otherwise None. This lets persona/config code pre-warm the engine a chosen voice needs, while leaving always-up engines like kokoro unmapped.

symbols.does: Lists the tts-* engine services that are currently up.
symbols.description: Filters loadable services to those whose id starts with 'tts-' and whose health endpoint answers. This is used by switch_to() to decide which persona voices must be evicted before loading a new one.

symbols.does: Swaps the resident persona TTS voice by unloading other tts-* engines and loading the target.
symbols.description: Validates that the requested id is a loadable tts-* engine, short-circuits if already resident, calls unload() on every other resident tts engine, then calls load() for the target. The load() budget gate provides the final safety check; the result includes the list of evicted engines.
